treaty_articles.text: Income derived by a resident of a Contracting State from immovable property situated in the other Contracting State may be taxed in that other State.
Dividends may be taxed in the State of residence; the source State may also tax them at a reduced rate, with relief by credit in the residence State.
Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State.
Salaries are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception.
Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
Income from immovable property may be taxed in the State where the property is situated.
Dividends may be taxed in the residence State; the source State may levy a reduced withholding tax.
Interest shall be taxable only in the State of residence of the beneficial owner.
Employment income is taxable where the employment is exercised, subject to the 183-day exception.
Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
Income from immovable property may be taxed in the State where the property is situated.
Dividends may be taxed in the residence State; the source State may levy a reduced withholding tax.
Interest shall be taxable only in the State of residence of the beneficial owner.
Employment income is taxable where the employment is exercised, subject to the 183-day exception.
Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State.
Salaries are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception.
Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State.
Salaries are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception.
Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State.
treaty_articles.source: UK–Ireland Double Taxation Convention (1976, as amended), Article 12

treaty_articles.text: Salaries are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception.
treaty_articles.source: UK–Ireland Double Taxation Convention (1976, as amended), Article 15

treaty_articles.text: Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
treaty_articles.source: UK–Ireland Double Taxation Convention (1976, as amended), Article 17

treaty_articles.text: Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State, subject to the Convention's anti-conduit and contingent-interest exceptions. Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(4)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.

Salaries are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception. Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(4)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.

Periodic private pensions beneficially owned by a resident of a Contracting State are taxable only in that State; lump-sum payments and government social-security benefits follow separate rules in Article 17(2)–(3). Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(4)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State.
treaty_articles.source: UK–South Africa Double Taxation Convention (2002, as amended by the 2010 Protocol), Article 11

treaty_articles.text: Salaries are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception.
treaty_articles.source: UK–South Africa Double Taxation Convention (2002, as amended by the 2010 Protocol), Article 14

treaty_articles.text: Private pensions are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
treaty_articles.source: UK–South Africa Double Taxation Convention (2002, as amended by the 2010 Protocol), Article 17

treaty_articles.text: Income from debt-claims (interest) arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State.
treaty_articles.source: UK–Saudi Arabia Double Taxation Convention (2007), Article 11

treaty_articles.text: Salaries (dependent personal services) are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day exception.
treaty_articles.source: UK–Saudi Arabia Double Taxation Convention (2007), Article 15

treaty_articles.text: Pensions and other similar remuneration are taxable only in the State of residence of the recipient.
treaty_articles.source: UK–Saudi Arabia Double Taxation Convention (2007), Article 18

treaty_articles.text: Interest arising in a Contracting State and beneficially owned by a resident of the other State shall be taxable only in that other State (0% at source), as amended by the 2013 Protocol; contingent interest and certain REMIC interest follow separate rules. Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(3)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
treaty_articles.source: Spain–United States Income Tax Convention (1990), as amended by the 2013 Protocol, Article 11

treaty_articles.text: Salaries, wages and other similar remuneration are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day (twelve-month period) exception in Article 16(2). Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(3)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
treaty_articles.source: Spain–United States Income Tax Convention (1990), Article 16

treaty_articles.text: Private pensions and other similar remuneration beneficially owned by a resident of a Contracting State in consideration of past employment are taxable only in that State (Article 20(1)(a)); social-security and similar public pensions follow separate rules in Article 20(1)(b). Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(3)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
treaty_articles.source: Spain–United States Income Tax Convention (1990), Article 20

treaty_articles.text: Interest derived and beneficially owned by a resident of a Contracting State shall be taxable only in that State (0% at source). Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(4)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
treaty_articles.source: Germany–United States Income Tax Convention (1989), as amended by the 2006 Protocol, Article 11

treaty_articles.text: Salaries, wages and other similar remuneration are taxable only in the State of residence unless the employment is exercised in the other State, subject to the 183-day (calendar year) exception in Article 15(2). Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(4)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
treaty_articles.source: Germany–United States Income Tax Convention (1989), Article 15

treaty_articles.text: Private pensions and other similar remuneration beneficially owned by a resident of a Contracting State in consideration of past employment are taxable only in that State (Article 18(1)); government-service pensions and social security follow separate rules in Article 19. Note: under the saving clause (Article 1(4)) the United States may still tax its citizens and residents as if the Convention had not entered into force.
treaty_articles.source: Germany–United States Income Tax Convention (1989), Article 18
